name: Mortons of Galway, Salthill, Galway., Salthill, Galway City
normalized_name: mortons of galway salthill galway salthill galway city
summary: Colleran Auctioneers are delighted to be favoured with the sale of one of Galway€TM much loved ,successful and award winning food emporiums. Mortons of Galway was established in 2003 by Eric Morton, continuing a family tradition started by his grandparents Charles & Esther Morton when they opened their first door in Dublin in 1934. Now known throughout the West as a quality food outlet, Mortons of Galway is known for the excellence of its food products which include an instore bakery, delicatessen, local fresh fruit and veg, butchers, fish counters and a very large selection of quality fine wines and foods. In the last two years the owner Eric has invested significantly in refitting the shop which has resulted in even further growth of this already very successful business. Located prominently in one of Irelands premier seaside resorts, Salthill is a booming area and is no longer just a summer destination. Salthill saw an unprecedented demand for property during Covid as everyone realised how much Salthill has to offer with every amenity on its doorstep from some of Galway€TM finest schools both primary and secondary, unlimited sports and recreational facilities and a large selection of award winning coffee shops and restaurants. Mortons is strategically located on the main access road in and out of Salthill and serves a large catchment area. Mortons of Galway is now being offered for sale as a very profitable going concern to include a large prime retail outlet with large yard for deliveries and outbuildings for additional storage. The newly refurbished retail unit consist of over 4,600 square feet of ground floor retail, ancillary service space and stores. On the first floor there is an additional 1,500 sqft which includes a new bakery and food preparation area, staff facilities and offices. The first floor also includes independent living consisting of a spacious 950sqft three bedroomed apartment. There is a large back yard with rear access for deliveries and a number of refrigerated stores. Above all Mortons of Galway offers a unique opportunity due to the owner€TM retirement to purchase a very successful and profitable business. Genuine enquires only by appointment with Don Colleran, Colleran Auctioneers, 21 Eyre Square, Galway 091 562293.

name: Nightingale Nursing Home, Lowville, Ahascragh, Ballinasloe, County Galway
normalized_name: nightingale nursing home lowville ahascragh ballinasloe county galway
summary: Offers in excess of 1,100,000.00 invited. Team McCullagh are delighted to present Nightingale Nursing Home, a rare and exceptional opportunity to acquire a long-established, purpose-built, and fully operational residential care facility in the heart of East Galway. Located in Lowville, just two minutes from Ahascragh village and ten minutes from Ballinasloe town, this single-storey nursing home sits on a c. 0.41 hectare (1 acre) site and comprises approximately 688 square metres (7,405 sq. ft) of modern, well-maintained accommodation. Built in 1996 and extended in 2005, Nightingale Nursing Home has played a vital role in the provision of elderly care services within the local community. The property is registered for 31 residents and features 13 twin bedrooms and 5 single bedrooms, including 2 with ensuite facilities. The home has been carefully designed to ensure comfort, safety, and accessibility for all residents. It includes spacious private and communal living areas, attractive landscaped gardens, and is fully compliant with current fire safety and accessibility standards. A B2 BER rating further reflects the quality and efficiency of the property. Nightingale Nursing Home is registered and fully compliant with HIQA, with current registration valid from November 2023 to November 2026. Nightingale Nursing Home provides 24-hour nursing care to residents aged 65 and over. Services include long-term residential care, short-term stays, respite care, and palliative care. Residents retain access to their own GP and benefit from regular visits by allied health professionals, including physiotherapists, occupational therapists, speech and language therapists, chiropodists, and dietitians, many of whom are available through HSE entitlements. The home`s care philosophy is rooted in a holistic, person-centred approach. It promotes independence, dignity, and a high quality of life for every resident, while also addressing their emotional, social, spiritual, and psychological well-being. Family involvement is an integral part of the care planning process, fostering a strong community environment within the home. Nightingale benefits from a strong reputation locally and receives referrals not only from the surrounding community but also from three referring hospitals, ensuring a consistent flow of potential residents. The home enjoys high occupancy levels, reflecting both demand for its services and the quality of care provided. This is a genuine retirement sale and presents a unique opportunity for healthcare operators or investors seeking to enter or expand within the residential care sector. With its excellent reputation, regulatory compliance, high occupancy, and strategic location in a growing region, Nightingale Nursing Home offers a turnkey solution with strong long-term potential. For further information or to arrange a confidential viewing, please contact Cathal Meares of Sheehy MearesTeam McCullagh on 086 140 9158

name: O'Connor Fuels, 2 St Endas Avenue, Tuam, Galway
normalized_name: o connor fuels 2 st endas avenue tuam galway
summary: FABULOUS OPPORTUNITY TO ACQUIRE AN ESTABLISHED FUEL BUSINESS INCORPORATING A GARDEN CENTRE, HARDWARE AND DIY LOCATED IN THE HEART OF TUAM TOWN. O'Connor Fuels has been successfully trading and is well established in the industry. The business will be sold as a going concern and accounts can be made available to serious interested parties once a Non Disclosure Agreement has been signed. O'Connor Fuels currently trades from Cloontooa Road on circa 0.3 of an acre, complete with a retail unit. The unique central location of such a sizable site is a great benefit to the business which brings with it a large percentage of return business. Already a very successful business model, there is huge potential to further diversify the business and in turn create an extra fee avenue. The retail unit located in the yard could be a fine example of this, giving options to sell a wide selection of commodities that will complement the current business model, with sales of solid fuel, garden centre, DIY, hardware, pet food, etc. There is planning for DIY & light hardware. As O'Connor Fuels is a trading business, viewings and all enquiries are strictly by appointment only. Contact our office today to avoid disappointment!

normalized_name: traditional irish pub in galway city
summary: Well-established pub with loyal local clientele and strong tourist trade This traditional Irish pub represents an exceptional opportunity to acquire a well-established business in the heart of Galway City. Located just steps from Eyre Square, the pub benefits from both strong local patronage and consistent tourist traffic throughout the year. The business has been family-owned and operated for over four decades, building a reputation for authentic Irish hospitality, quality food, and traditional music sessions. The property features a classic pub interior with original fixtures, a full commercial kitchen, and outdoor seating area.
